title: Accelerator Participation Agreement
description: Terms for a startup joining an incubator/accelerator program, exchanging workspace and mentoring for a small equity percentage.
whatsCovered: Accelerator funding cash grant and equity transfer splits
Program participation guidelines, workshops, and demo day
Accelerator rights to participate in future rounds
Intellectual property preservation by the startup
Termination from program for failure to participate
whenToUse: Use this agreement to structure terms when a startup joins an accelerator program that provides funding, office space, and advisors.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: ACCELERATOR PARTICIPATION AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Accelerator Participation Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Accelerator Program Equity
clauses.body: Startup joins Accelerator {accelerator_name} for a {program_months}-month incubator program, transferring {equity_grant}% common stock in exchange.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is a standard equity exchange for accelerators?
faq.answer: Most top accelerators take between 5% and 10% of common stock in exchange for program access, workspace, and initial cash.

faq.question: Does the accelerator own my IP?
faq.answer: No, the startup retains 100% of its intellectual property. The accelerator only receives equity shares.
